title: Lattice — Topology & Capability Document
description: An OpenAPI-style, vendor-publishable document describing a site's device topology (a graph of nodes connected by typed directed relationships) and the read/control capabilities each node offers, with concrete data-driven bindings and ranked access paths. A producer (a cloud integration or the gateway) emits a conformant doc; any consumer ingests it generically with no per-provider code. Scale-free: the same constructs describe a cell, a device, a site, or a city.

topologyVersion.description: Version of THIS specification the doc conforms to (semver).

docVersion.description: Monotonic instance version of THIS document. Bumps on any change, including capability `ref` (re)assignment. The data-plane protobuf carries `doc_version`; a consumer decodes telemetry/encodes control against the doc of the matching docVersion (re-reading the retained topology on an unknown version).

id.description: Stable identifier for this document / site / fragment.

scope.description: 'fragment' = one producer's slice (the consumer merges fragments by node identity); 'site' = an already-merged graph.

deviceTypes.description: Reusable device-type descriptors (the components/$ref equivalent): a fingerprint plus capability/binding templates, referenced by node.deviceType.

provider.description: Provider/transport identity backing this producer's access paths.

id.description: Stable identity (e.g. a serial). THE merge key across producers — the same id from two producers is one node with both their access paths.

deviceType.description: Optional key referencing a deviceTypes[] descriptor.

accessPaths.description: Ways this producer can reach the node. The consumer merges + ranks all producers' access paths to the same id.

nodeKind.description: Domain-agnostic node class. Vendor/domain extensions use a namespace prefix, e.g. 'x-acme:thing'. 'structural' = a pure aggregator/virtual node (e.g. a plant or a city) with no telemetry of its own.

attributes.description: Free-form node attributes referenced by routing, grouping and constraints.

geo.description: Optional geospatial location — needed at building/district/city scale.

accessPath.description: A way to reach this node's capabilities via a provider/transport.

preference.description: Higher = preferred; the consumer fails over to the next-highest on unavailability.

aggregate.description: When (and with what priority) this node serves as the aggregate read/control point for its children, instead of routing to each child. Replaces hardcoded primary-selection with data.

minChildren.description: Qualifies only if the configured child-count over `over` >= this. e.g. EMS=0 (always primary), Gateway=2 (only when it fronts >1 unit).

priority.description: Higher wins when several aggregators qualify (e.g. EMS > Gateway).

capabilityOffer.description: A capability this node offers on a given access path, with read and/or control binding(s), aggregation semantics and constraints. The same capability may appear multiple times on one node (one per access path) — the consumer ranks them by access-path preference (this is how 'control via gateway OR cloud, with fallback' is expressed).

ref.description: Stable small integer (the data-plane `cap_ref`) identifying this (node, capability) on telemetry/control. Keyed by node+capability — multiple access-path offers for the same capability SHARE one ref (the consumer picks the access path). Stable within a docVersion.

accessPath.description: id of the node's accessPath that serves this offer.

groupBy.description: Attribute name partitioning a multi-element read into per-group results (e.g. 'phase' for grid voltage).

shape.description: How the device accepts a control intent for this capability: a numeric value effective now (setpoint), an enum/on-off effective now (switch), or a time-windowed plan applied atomically (schedule). Required on an offer that carries a `control` binding. The schedule payload is an ordered list of slots {start,end,mode,charge_power_limit?,target_soc?,reserve_soc?} + a default mode — a control-time contract (see the control-model design doc), not part of this document.

controlGroup.description: Name of a coupled-write group. Capabilities on the same node sharing a controlGroup resolve and execute as ONE atomic operation (e.g. Solax (mode,target,power)); the resolver gathers all members' values, reading current state for any the consumer didn't supply.

tier.description: Conformance tier of this control offer: 1 = declarative binding (zero provider code, e.g. Modbus/CID); 2 = provider-implemented (imperative: schedule rebuild, coupled mode call, OAuth cloud write). Required on an offer that carries a `control` binding.

capabilityName.description: Capability identity, qualified by device class as 'class.function' (the merge/resolve key). The class reflects the function's domain, not the node's kind (a gateway may offer 'battery.charge_power_limit' AND 'meter.grid_power'). Qualifying by class prevents collisions a bare name causes — e.g. a battery's charge power (W) vs an EV charger's charge current (A) are 'battery.charge_power_limit' and 'ev_charger.charge_current_limit', never grouped. Vendor/domain extensions namespace further (e.g. 'x-acme:foo'); a capability name MUST NOT name a vendor or device. Core set in examples.

binding.description: The concrete 'how to read/write this on this access path': protocol, address, op, encoding and a value transform. An adapter executes it; the model needs no per-model code. This is what replaces hardcoded register maps.

address.description: Register number/range, API endpoint/field, OCPP action, etc.

count.description: # elements/registers — vector reads (per-unit SOC) or slot start+end.

readModifyWrite.description: true if executing this control op requires reading current device state, merging the new value, and writing the whole object back (e.g. Fox schedule rebuild) — the executor read-modify-writes rather than writing a scalar.

transform.description: Named, parameterised value transform (raw <-> engineering units). Core kinds are GENERIC; vendor-specifics live in a binding's parameters (e.g. a `ref` to a node parameter), or — as a last resort — a namespaced `x-<vendor>:` extension kind. A vendor name MUST NOT appear as a core kind (same rule as capability names).

kind.description: A core registry kind, or a namespaced `x-<vendor>:` extension.

valueOrRef.description: A transform parameter: a literal number, or a reference to a declared node parameter with an optional constant factor.

ref.description: Name of a node `parameters` entry; resolved at execution time.

factor.description: Constant multiplier on the referenced value (e.g. capacity × 0.5).

nodeParameters.description: Already-canonical node values a transform `ref` resolves against at execution time. A referenced value must itself be resolvable from a non-referencing binding (no cycles, which holds by construction while parameters are literals); if unavailable at runtime the binding yields no value (fail-closed).

reducer.description: How multiple read sources (or vector elements) combine. 'representative' = redundant value, read one. 'spread' = max-min (e.g. cell imbalance).

distribution.description: How one control intent maps onto multiple targets.

relationType.description: Directed edge, read subject->object, with an implied named inverse.

constraints.description: Bounds/valid-set/interlocks resolution must respect. May be static or a derived expression.

max.description: A number, or a derived expression like 'min(capacity/2, rated)'.

scope.description: site = e.g. max grid import, G98/G99 export limit.

deviceType.description: Reusable descriptor: a fingerprint (recognition rules — the discovery schema as data) plus capability/binding templates and a default aggregate. Instances reference it via node.deviceType. Keyed by (make, model, firmware); a firmware change is a new descriptor version, not a per-node rewrite.

fingerprint.description: How to recognise a device as this type (used by discovered producers, e.g. the gateway).

probe.description: e.g. an input register that must be > 0.
